articles.title: Health ministry deploys teams to assess service delivery across the country
articles.summary: The Ministry of Health has deployed teams to several locations across the country to assess health service delivery and strengthen the health system in order to improve the delivery of quality healthcare services. According to a statement issued by the ministry this morning, the supervision mission is being coordinated by the Project Management Unit (PMU), […] The post Health ministry deploys teams to assess service delivery across the country appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing challenges in healthcare delivery since its independence in 2011, exacerbated by civil conflict that erupted in 2013. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to stabilize the nation, yet health services remain severely underfunded and fragmented. The Ministry of Health's current initiative reflects an urgent need to address these systemic issues and improve access to quality healthcare amidst ongoing humanitarian crises.

articles.title: وزارة الانتاج والمسجل التجاري بالقضارف يبحثان توفيق اوضاع الشركات الزراعية استعدادا للموسم الزراعي الجديد
articles.summary: عقد المدير العام لوزارة الإنتاج والموارد الاقتصادية بولاية القضارف، المهندس عمار عبد الله سليمان اليوم اجتماعاً بمكاتب إدارة التسجيلات التجارية بالولاية، مع المستشار العام عبد المنعم محمد عبد القيوم رئيس إدارة التسجيلات التجارية، وذلك لمناقشة عدد من القضايا المتعلقة بتنظيم وتوفيق أوضاع الشركات والأعمال الزراعية بالولاية. وتناول الاجتماع الجوانب القانونية الخاصة بتسجيل الأعمال الزراعية، سواء …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الاجتماعات في وقت حرج لولاية القضارف، حيث تواجه البلاد تحديات اقتصادية كبيرة نتيجة النزاع المستمر منذ عام 2019 بين القوات المسلحة السودانية وقوات الدعم السريع. هذا الصراع أثر سلباً على القطاع الزراعي، الذي يعد أساسياً للاقتصاد السوداني، مما يجعل تنظيم وتوفيق أوضاع الشركات الزراعية أمراً ضرورياً لضمان استدامة الإنتاج الزراعي مع اقتراب الموسم الزراعي الجديد.

articles.title: البنك المركزي يعلن اكتمال التكامل بين النظام المصرفي الأساسي ونظام الدفع ايصالي
articles.summary: اكد بنك السودان المركزي اكتمال أعمال التكامل بين النظام المصرفي الأساسي لبنك السودان المركزي ونظام الدفع الإلكتروني بوزارة المالية (إيصالي). وقال أبوبكر الحسين رئيس فريق تبادل المعلومات مع وزارة المالية أن ذلك يعتبر خطوة مهمة نحو تعزيز التكامل بين الجهتين، حيث أصبح تبادل البيانات يتم إلكترونيًا، مع إتاحة معلومات حسابات الحكومة الرئيسية بصورة لحظية ومحمية، …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطوة في سياق جهود بنك السودان المركزي لتعزيز الاستقرار المالي في البلاد بعد سنوات من الأزمات الاقتصادية والسياسية. منذ توقيع اتفاق جوبا للسلام في أكتوبر 2020، تسعى الحكومة الانتقالية إلى تحسين الشفافية المالية وتعزيز الثقة في النظام المصرفي، مما يجعل التكامل بين النظامين المصرفي ونظام الدفع الإلكتروني خطوة استراتيجية في هذا الاتجاه.

articles.title: South Sudan rights activist appeals for protection over alleged death threats
articles.summary: South Sudanese human rights activist Ter Manyang Gatwech has appealed to foreign missions and human The post South Sudan rights activist appeals for protection over alleged death threats appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: Ter Manyang Gatwech's appeal comes amid escalating violence and human rights abuses in South Sudan, particularly following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed at ending the civil war. The ongoing power struggles between President Salva Kiir and opposition leader Riek Machar have intensified threats against activists. Recent reports indicate a surge in targeted attacks against those advocating for justice, highlighting the precarious situation for human rights defenders in the region.

articles.title: Ethiopia Nears Restructuring Deal with Bondholders
articles.summary: The Ministry of Finance announced today it has reached an agreement in principle with an Ad Hoc Committee representing investors behind its one-billion-dollar Eurobond. The agreement, which features terms for subscription rights for a new international bond to be issued in the future, is the second such deal between Ethiopia and bondholders this year, and […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant economic challenges exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020 and led to widespread humanitarian crises. In response to its mounting debt and economic pressures, the Ethiopian government has sought restructuring agreements with bondholders, including a previous deal in March 2023. This latest agreement with the Ad Hoc Committee marks a critical step in stabilizing the economy and restoring investor confidence amid ongoing political tensions and recovery efforts.

articles.title: اتفاق تعاون بين جامعة الجزيرة ومؤسسة مدارك القطرية
articles.summary: وقّعت جامعة الجزيرة ومؤسسة مدارك للبحوث والخدمات الجامعية بدولة قطر عبر تقنية الإتصال المرئي (Google Meet) صباح اليوم إتفاقية تعاون أكاديمي لتعزيز التعاون الأكاديمي والترويج لبرامج الدراسات العليا بكلية الإعلام على المستويين الإقليمي والدولي . حيث وقّع عن جامعة الجزيرة الدكتور ربيع أحمد أسد عميد كلية الدراسات العليا ووقّع عن مؤسسة مدارك البروفيسور إبراهيم أحمد …
articles.context: تأتي اتفاقية التعاون بين جامعة الجزيرة ومؤسسة مدارك في سياق سعي السودان لتعزيز التعليم العالي والبحث العلمي في ظل التحديات السياسية والاقتصادية التي يواجهها. بعد الثورة السودانية في 2019، تسعى الجامعات إلى بناء شراكات دولية لتعزيز برامجها الأكاديمية، مما يعكس رغبة الحكومة الانتقالية في تحسين جودة التعليم وتوسيع آفاق التعاون مع المؤسسات التعليمية في قطر، خاصة في مجال الإعلام.

articles.title: الشمالية: 90% نسبة أداء التأمين الصحي خلال النصف الأول من 2026
articles.summary: اكدت مدير الصندوق القومي للتأمين الصحي فرع الولاية الشمالية دكتورة شيراز عمر حجازي ان نسبة أداء الصندوق للنصف الأول من العام 2026م بلغت 90% واشارت الي ان التغطية السكانية بالولاية وصلت لأكثر من 83%. واضافت لدي مخاطبتها اليوم بمباني الصندوق بدنقلا فاتحة أعمال الدورة التدريبية بعنوان فن الكتابة الصحفية والتصوير الاحترافي والتي تنظمها وحدة الإعلام …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الإحصائيات في سياق جهود الحكومة السودانية لتعزيز نظام الرعاية الصحية بعد سنوات من النزاع والصراعات التي أثرت سلباً على الخدمات الأساسية. منذ توقيع اتفاقية جوبا للسلام في أكتوبر 2020، تسعى الحكومة إلى تحسين الخدمات الصحية في المناطق النائية مثل الولاية الشمالية، حيث كانت التغطية الصحية تمثل تحدياً كبيراً.

articles.title: موجة الحر.. 10 حيل سريعة وفعالة لتبريد جسمك

articles.context: تواجه السودان صيفًا حارًا بشكل خاص هذا العام، حيث شهدت البلاد ارتفاعًا غير مسبوق في درجات الحرارة بسبب التغيرات المناخية والنزاعات المستمرة. الصراع الدائر منذ أبريل 2023 بين القوات المسلحة السودانية وقوات الدعم السريع أدى إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية، مما يجعل الحفاظ على صحة السكان ورفاهيتهم أمرًا حيويًا. في ظل هذه الظروف، تصبح استراتيجيات التبريد ضرورية لتفادي المخاطر الصحية المرتبطة بالحرارة.

Western Equatoria State has been a focal point for various political factions, and President Kiir's decision to replace officials may reflect efforts to consolidate power and address local grievances amid rising ethnic tensions and economic challenges in the region.

articles.title: SPLM-IO questions election timetable, cites unmet peace deal provisions
articles.summary: A faction of the opposition SPLM-IO loyal to detained First Vice President Riek Machar questioned The post SPLM-IO questions election timetable, cites unmet peace deal provisions appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The SPLM-IO, led by Riek Machar, has been a key player in South Sudan's turbulent political landscape since the civil war erupted in 2013. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to establish peace and a transitional government, but many provisions remain unfulfilled, leading to tensions. With national elections scheduled for 2024, the SPLM-IO's concerns highlight the fragile state of the peace process and the potential for renewed conflict.

articles.title: Activist calls for establishment of affordable rehab centers in all states
articles.summary: A women’s rights advocate has called for the establishment of affordable rehabilitation centers in all the states and three administrative areas to help address rising drug abuse among young people. Adut Christina, Executive Director of the South Sudan Women Empowerment Network, said communities across South Sudan continue to face challenges in dealing with drug abuse. […] The post Activist calls for establishment of affordable rehab centers in all states appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has been grappling with the aftermath of its civil war, which officially ended in 2018 with the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan. Despite the peace deal, ongoing instability and economic challenges have exacerbated youth unemployment and drug abuse. The South Sudan Women Empowerment Network, led by Adut Christina, aims to address these pressing issues as the government struggles to implement effective social services amidst the political turmoil.

articles.title: Students urge govt to reform scholarship programme after minister’s criticism
articles.summary: South Sudanese students studying in Morocco under a government scholarship programme have urged authorities to The post Students urge govt to reform scholarship programme after minister’s criticism appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: South Sudan's government has faced criticism for its management of scholarship programs, particularly after the 2011 independence from Sudan, which left many students struggling for educational opportunities abroad. The recent comments from the Minister of Higher Education, which highlighted inefficiencies, have intensified calls for reform among South Sudanese students in Morocco. This situation is compounded by ongoing economic challenges and political instability in South Sudan, which hinder educational access and quality.

articles.context: تأتي هذه الحادثة في وقت حساس حيث تشهد الولايات المتحدة تصاعدًا في حوادث العنف المسلح، مما يثير القلق بشأن السلامة العامة خلال الفعاليات الكبرى مثل كأس العالم. كما أن كأس العالم 2026 يمثل حدثًا عالميًا يجمع بين عدة دول، بما في ذلك الولايات المتحدة، مما يزيد من أهمية تعزيز الأمن في الأماكن العامة. هذا الحادث قد يؤثر على خطط تنظيم الفعاليات الرياضية الكبرى في المستقبل.

articles.title: TPLF Rejects EU Criticism, Accuses Federal Government of Violating Peace Deal
articles.summary: The Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) has rejected European Union accusations that it is undermining Ethiopia’s 2022 peace agreement, instead accusing the federal government of violating the accord and warning that the Tigray region faces a growing risk of renewed conflict. In a statement issued in response to the European Union delegation’s call for the […]
articles.context: The TPLF and the Ethiopian federal government signed a peace agreement on November 2, 2022, to end a brutal two-year conflict in Tigray that resulted in widespread humanitarian crises. Tensions have persisted as both sides accuse each other of failing to uphold the terms of the agreement, particularly regarding disarmament and humanitarian access. The EU's recent criticism highlights the fragile political dynamics and the potential for renewed violence in the region.

articles.title: كاتي بيري تُعرب عن امتنانها لحبيبها : هذه هي الحياة التي أستحقها
articles.summary: شاركت النجمة الأمريكية كاتي بيري مقطع فيديو عبر حسابها على إنستجرام، تضمن مجموعة من اللقطات التي وثّقت أبرز محطات حياتها خلال الأسابيع الماضية، وذلك على أنغام أغنيتها الجديدة Watch It Burn. وتضمّن الفيديو عدة مشاهد جمعتها برئيس الوزراء الكندي السابق جاستن ترودو، الذي ترتبط به عاطفيًا منذ أواخر عام 2025، وفق ما أوردته تقارير إعلامية. …
articles.context: في ظل الأزمات السياسية والاقتصادية التي تشهدها السودان، حيث تتواصل النزاعات المسلحة منذ أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع، تبرز قصص المشاهير كاتي بيري وجاستن ترودو كرموز للسلام والتضامن. تأتي هذه العلاقة في وقت يحتاج فيه السودان إلى دعم دولي ورموز إيجابية، مما يسلط الضوء على أهمية الروابط الإنسانية في مواجهة التحديات الكبرى.

articles.title: بسمة بوسيل من ستار أكاديمى إلى عالم الأزياء وعودة للغناء مرة أخرى
articles.summary: بسمة بوسيل فنانة مغربية من مواليد 10 مايو 1991 في ورزازات بالمغرب. درست الطب البشري لكنها لم تكمل دراستها بسبب دخولها المجال الفني. البداية شاركت بسمة بوسيل في الموسم السادس من برنامج ستار أكاديمي عام 2009 وحصلت على المركز الثاني. كانت أكثر الطلاب غناء في البرايمات حيث قدمت 45 أغنية منفردة ودويتو وجماعية حتى النهائيات. …
articles.context: تأتي قصة بسمة بوسيل في سياق متغيرات المشهد الفني في المنطقة، حيث تسعى الفنانات المغربيات إلى تعزيز وجودهن في صناعة الأزياء والموسيقى. في ظل التوترات السياسية والاجتماعية التي تشهدها دول مثل السودان، يمثل نجاح بوسيل نموذجًا ملهمًا للشباب في المنطقة، خاصة بعد اتفاق جوبا للسلام في 2020 الذي يسعى إلى تعزيز الاستقرار والتنمية الثقافية.

articles.title: أول قرار لأشرف حكيمي بعد تأييد إحالته إلى المحاكمة في فرنسا
articles.summary: اتخذ النجم المغربي أشرف حكيمي المحترف في صفوف باريس سان جيرمان، أول إجراء قانوني له بعد تأييد إحالته إلى المحاكمة بتهمة الاغتصاب. وصرحت فاني بولين، محامية حكيمي، لوكالة “رويترز” بأن موكلها سيستأنف القرار الذي يؤيد إحالته للمحاكمة بتهمة اغتصاب أمام محكمة النقض الفرنسية، وهي أعلى هيئة قضائية في البلاد، مؤكدة عزمه مواصلة الدفاع عن براءته …
articles.context: تأتي قضية أشرف حكيمي في وقت حساس للرياضة في فرنسا، حيث تتزايد المخاوف بشأن الاعتداءات الجنسية في المجتمع الرياضي. بعد حركة #MeToo، شهدت البلاد زيادة في الوعي حول هذه القضايا، مما أدى إلى محاكمات بارزة. كما أن حكيمي، الذي يمثل المغرب في كأس العالم، يواجه ضغوطاً إضافية من وسائل الإعلام والجماهير، مما يسلط الضوء على التوترات بين الشهرة والعدالة في السياق الرياضي.

articles.title: Resumption of Talks on Tigray is Urgent, Says EU
articles.summary: The European Union delegation in Ethiopia has urged for the resumption of talks to resolve tensions in Tigray. “A resumption of talks on the concrete implementation of CoHA by all sides is urgent. We fully support the mediation efforts led by the AU’s High Representative President Obasanjo to achieve peace and stability,” said the EU, […]
articles.context: The Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020 between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF), has resulted in a humanitarian crisis and significant loss of life. The signing of the Pretoria Agreement on November 2, 2022, aimed to cease hostilities, but implementation challenges persist. The EU's call for renewed talks highlights the urgency of addressing ongoing tensions and ensuring compliance with the agreement amid regional instability and humanitarian needs.

articles.title: فى يوم ميلادها.. محطات صنعت مشوار نوال الزغبى الفنى
articles.summary: تحتفل اليوم الإثنين النجمة اللبنانية نوال الزغبي بعيد ميلادها، بعدما نجحت على مدار أكثر من ثلاثة عقود في ترسيخ مكانتها كواحدة من أبرز نجمات الغناء في الوطن العربي، بفضل صوتها المميز وحضورها اللافت وقدرتها الدائمة على مواكبة التطور الفني. وتمكنت نوال الزغبي منذ انطلاقتها في تسعينيات القرن الماضي من تحقيق نجاحات كبيرة، حيث قدمت عشرات …
articles.context: تحتفل نوال الزغبي بعيد ميلادها في وقت يشهد فيه العالم العربي، بما في ذلك لبنان، تحديات اقتصادية وسياسية كبيرة. الأزمة الاقتصادية اللبنانية المستمرة منذ 2019 أثرت على صناعة الموسيقى والفنون، مما يجعل نجاح نوال الزغبي في الحفاظ على مكانتها الفنية وسط هذه الظروف أكثر أهمية. كما أن تفاعلها مع جمهورها عبر وسائل التواصل الاجتماعي يعكس قدرة الفنانين على التكيف مع الأزمات والتواصل مع محبيهم.

articles.title: Nairobi lawyer sues to stop controversial new NTSA traffic rules
articles.summary: The petition is pending directions on the urgency application.
articles.context: The new NTSA traffic rules, introduced in September 2023, have sparked widespread debate in Kenya, with critics arguing they disproportionately affect low-income drivers. The rules come amid ongoing tensions between the government and transport unions, particularly following the 2022 protests against rising fuel prices and vehicle licensing fees. This lawsuit reflects broader concerns about governance and public accountability in the wake of these contentious reforms.

articles.title: “Degrees without skills”: Ting Mayai, Prof. John Akec clash over University of Juba’s quality

articles.context: The University of Juba, established in 1977, has faced significant challenges due to South Sudan's ongoing conflict since its independence in 2011. The nation grapples with a dire need for skilled professionals amid a struggling education system, exacerbated by economic instability and political fragmentation. The clash between Dr. Ting Mayai and Prof. John Akec highlights the urgent discourse on educational reform and workforce readiness, critical for South Sudan's recovery and development.

articles.title: How Editing Alongside CGI Enhances the Magical Feel in Movies
articles.summary: Movies have always been a gateway to worlds beyond our imagination. Whether audiences are watching dragons soar through the skies, enchanted forests come alive, or superheroes wield extraordinary powers, the sense of wonder comes from more than just visual effects. While computer-generated imagery (CGI) often receives the spotlight, film editing plays an equally important role […]
articles.context: In Ethiopia, the film industry is experiencing a renaissance, fueled by a growing interest in storytelling and visual arts. The 2020 establishment of the Ethiopian Film Industry Development Council aims to promote local filmmakers and enhance production quality. Amid ongoing political tensions and conflict in regions like Tigray, the rise of CGI and advanced editing techniques in Ethiopian cinema offers a means for cultural expression and unity, reflecting the country's diverse narratives and aspirations.

articles.title: U.S. furniture Giant Ashley Opens Second Ethiopian Branch
articles.summary: Yusra Home, the exclusive distributor of Ashley Furniture in Ethiopia, has officially opened its second showroom in the country. This move is stated to further strengthen the American furniture giant’s footprint in the East African market. The new branch, opened in the Gourd Shola area of Addis Ababa, follows the successful opening of the first […]
articles.context: The opening of Ashley Furniture's second showroom in Ethiopia comes amid a recovering economy following the Tigray conflict, which lasted from November 2020 to November 2022. The Ethiopian government is actively seeking foreign investment to bolster economic growth and stability, as outlined in its Homegrown Economic Reform agenda launched in 2020. This expansion by a major U.S. retailer signals renewed confidence in the Ethiopian market and its potential for international business.

Yet an equally important question deserves attention: has the cure become more damaging […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant economic challenges following the outbreak of the Tigray conflict in November 2020, which exacerbated inflation and disrupted supply chains. As the country navigates post-conflict recovery and seeks to attract foreign investment, the effectiveness of these policies is under intense scrutiny.

articles.context: تأتي حفلات مهرجان موازين في وقت حساس للمنطقة، حيث تشهد العديد من دول شمال أفريقيا، بما في ذلك السودان، تحديات سياسية واقتصادية. بعد الثورة السودانية في 2019، لا تزال البلاد تواجه صراعات داخلية، مما يجعل الفعاليات الثقافية مثل مهرجان موازين فرصة لتعزيز الروابط الاجتماعية والفنية بين الدول العربية. كما أن تامر حسني يُعتبر رمزاً ثقافياً يجذب الجماهير في ظل هذه الظروف المتغيرة.

articles.title: Yakani calls for inclusive dialogue ahead of elections
articles.summary: Civil society leader Edmund Yakani is calling on the country’s political leaders to engage in an inclusive national dialogue before conducting elections scheduled for the end of this year. Yakani, who is the Executive Director of the Community Empowerment for Progress Organization (CEPO), warns that a lack of consensus could undermine prospects for a peaceful […] The post Yakani calls for inclusive dialogue ahead of elections appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan is approaching elections scheduled for December 2023, following a fragile peace agreement signed in 2018 that aimed to end years of civil war. The political landscape remains tense, with ongoing disputes among key leaders, including President Salva Kiir and opposition leader Riek Machar. Edmund Yakani's call for inclusive dialogue highlights concerns that without broad consensus, the elections could exacerbate existing tensions and lead to renewed violence.

articles.title: Cabinet approves over $3 million for national teams
articles.summary: The Council of Ministers has approved more than 3 million US dollars to support the national basketball team, the Bright Stars, ahead of major international competitions, including the FIBA Basketball World Cup 2027 African Qualifiers next month. The budget, amounting to 3,087,440 million dollars, was approved yesterday during a cabinet meeting chaired by President Salva […] The post Cabinet approves over $3 million for national teams appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing political instability since its independence in 2011, marked by a civil war that lasted until 2018. The recent peace agreement, signed in September 2018, aimed to stabilize the country, but challenges remain. The government's investment in the national basketball team reflects a broader effort to promote national unity and international representation, especially as the Bright Stars prepare for the FIBA Basketball World Cup 2027 African Qualifiers starting next month.

articles.title: Yei authorities destroy illegal drugs and alcohol in crackdown on illicit substances
articles.summary: Authorities in Yei River County have destroyed illegal drugs and unlicensed alcoholic products as part of a crackdown on illicit substances. In an operation conducted on Friday, authorities confiscated and burned alcoholic drinks and illegal drugs seized during inspections in the market. The operation was carried out jointly by county authorities, security agencies, health officials […] The post Yei authorities destroy illegal drugs and alcohol in crackdown on illicit substances appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: Yei River County, part of South Sudan, has faced significant instability due to ongoing conflicts and the presence of various armed groups since the civil war began in 2013. The 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed to reduce violence and promote stability, yet illegal drug and alcohol trade remains rampant, exacerbating social issues. The recent crackdown reflects local authorities' efforts to restore order and public health amid ongoing challenges in governance and security.

articles.title: South Sudan, US sign $146 million health deal
articles.summary: The government of South Sudan and the United States have signed a partnership agreement on health cooperation worth 146 million US dollars aimed at strengthening the country’s health system and improving resilience against disease outbreaks. The Memorandum of Understanding was signed on Friday in Juba by the Undersecretary in the Ministry of Health, Dr. Oromo […] The post South Sudan, US sign $146 million health deal appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing challenges since its independence from Sudan in 2011, including civil conflict and humanitarian crises that have strained its health infrastructure. The signing of this $146 million health deal on October 20, 2023, comes amid efforts to stabilize the country post-conflict and improve public health systems, particularly in light of recent disease outbreaks like cholera and malaria. The partnership reflects the U.S.'s commitment to supporting South Sudan's recovery and development.

articles.title: Abyei expands affordable maize distribution to Alal and Mijak counties
articles.summary: The Abyei Special Administrative Area has expanded the distribution of subsidised maize to Alal and Mijak counties as part of efforts to cushion residents from rising food prices. Authorities said maize is being sold at 12,000 South Sudanese Pounds per cylindrical tin container with a capacity of 4 kilograms, below the current market price of […] The post Abyei expands affordable maize distribution to Alal and Mijak counties appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The Abyei region, contested between Sudan and South Sudan, has faced ongoing tensions since the 2011 secession of South Sudan. The 2005 Comprehensive Peace Agreement aimed to resolve these disputes, but the area remains a flashpoint for conflict. Rising food prices, exacerbated by economic instability and the impact of climate change, have prompted local authorities to implement subsidized maize distribution to alleviate food insecurity in Alal and Mijak counties, highlighting the urgent need for humanitarian support.

articles.title: Opinion| South Sudan: 15 years of independence, US$70 billion in oil wealth, and GDP per capita cut by two-thirds
articles.summary: For years, warning signs have accumulated over the management of South Sudan’s oil revenues. Resources The post Opinion| South Sudan: 15 years of independence, US$70 billion in oil wealth, and GDP per capita cut by two-thirds appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: Since gaining independence from Sudan in 2011, South Sudan has struggled with internal conflict, exacerbated by a civil war that erupted in 2013, leading to a humanitarian crisis. Despite possessing vast oil reserves, the country has faced rampant corruption and mismanagement, with the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan failing to stabilize the political landscape. As a result, GDP per capita has plummeted, highlighting the dire economic situation.

articles.title: Primate Badi arrives in Rumbek ahead of Eastern Bahr el Ghazal Bishop enthronement
articles.summary: The primate of the Episcopal Church of South Sudan, Justin Badi Arama, has travelled to Rumbek, the capital of Lakes State, ahead of the inauguration of the new bishop of the Eastern Bahr el Ghazal Internal Province. articles.context: The enthronement of Bishop Peter Marial Agok comes at a critical time for South Sudan, as the country grapples with ongoing tensions following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed at ending years of civil war. The Episcopal Church plays a vital role in promoting peace and reconciliation among communities, particularly in regions like Eastern Bahr el Ghazal, which have been affected by violence and displacement. Bishop Agok's leadership is seen as pivotal for fostering unity in this fragile context.

articles.title: Biara Wepukhulu Visits Children Orphaned by Bulambuli Landslides, Vows Greater Support to Secure Their Future
articles.summary: Hope and excitement filled Yoweri Museveni Primary School in Namatala, Mbale District, as Hon. Biara Emmanuel Wepukhulu paid The post Biara Wepukhulu Visits Children Orphaned by Bulambuli Landslides, Vows Greater Support to Secure Their Future appeared first on The Nile Post .
articles.context: The Bulambuli landslides in Uganda, which occurred in June 2022, resulted in significant loss of life and displacement, leaving many children orphaned. This tragedy highlights the ongoing challenges of climate change and inadequate disaster preparedness in the region. Hon. Biara Emmanuel Wepukhulu's visit comes amid efforts to address the humanitarian needs of affected communities and reflects the broader political dynamics in Uganda, where government responses to natural disasters are increasingly scrutinized.

articles.title: Diverging Assessments Delay Ethiopia’s Debt Restructuring Prospects
articles.summary: “Ethiopia is left to navigate between hardening positions on both sides”: MoF Advisor Inconsistencies between IMF and creditor assessments of the Ethiopian economy is proving a hurdle in Ethiopia’s efforts to secure debt treatment under the G20 Common Framework, according to the Paris Club’s 2025 report. The report highlights the agreement-in-principle reached in March 2025 […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant economic challenges following a devastating civil conflict that erupted in November 2020, exacerbating its debt crisis. The G20 Common Framework, established in 2020 to address debt vulnerabilities in low-income countries, has become a focal point for Ethiopia's negotiations. The IMF's differing assessments from creditor nations, including the Paris Club, complicate these efforts, particularly as Ethiopia seeks to stabilize its economy while managing ongoing tensions in the Tigray region and beyond.

articles.title: Eritrea’s “Low-Cost” Destabilization Strategy Poses Serious Security Threat: Getachew Reda
articles.summary: US embassy urges dialogue in Tigray Eritrea’s primary security threat to Ethiopia no longer lies in conventional warfare but in organizing, financing, and coordinating armed groups operating inside the country, says Getachew Reda, advisor to the Prime Minister and former leader of the Tigray Interim Administration. Speaking at the Annual National Security Conference of the […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Ethiopia, particularly since the Tigray War erupted in November 2020, has shifted the dynamics of regional security. Eritrea's involvement in supporting Ethiopian federal forces against the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) has raised concerns about its influence in destabilizing Ethiopia through proxy groups. The 2022 Pretoria Agreement aimed to end hostilities, but tensions remain high, with Eritrea's alleged support for armed factions posing a renewed threat to Ethiopia's stability.

articles.title: Airbus Analysis Spotlights ET African Market Growth, Identifies Lucrative Routes
articles.summary: Ethiopian Airlines is making strides in capturing market share from Gulf and regional competitors in routes connecting African hubs to centers like London and Dubai, according to an analysis published by European aerospace giant Airbus. The report, dubbed ‘Exploring the Horizons: A Study of Unserved Air Routes to, from and within Sub-Saharan Africa’ and published […]
articles.context: Ethiopian Airlines has been pivotal in enhancing Ethiopia's position as a regional aviation hub, especially following the African Union's relocation to Addis Ababa in 2013. Amid ongoing tensions in the Tigray region and the broader geopolitical competition with Gulf carriers like Emirates and Qatar Airways, the airline's expansion into unserved routes is crucial for economic recovery and regional connectivity post-COVID-19. The recent African Continental Free Trade Area agreement further underscores the importance of improved air travel in boosting intra-African trade.

articles.title: Afreximbank Urges Focus on Intra-African Trade, Showcases Rising Payment System Adoption
articles.summary: East African countries are accelerating the adoption of local currency settlement for cross border trade through the Pan-African Payment and Settlement System (PAPSS) as the continent pushes to reduce dependence on the US dollar and euro, according to the African Trade Report 2026. The report published by Afreximbank identifies digital payment infrastructure as a central […]
articles.context: The push for local currency settlement in East Africa is particularly significant as the region grapples with economic challenges exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic and ongoing conflicts, such as the Tigray War in Ethiopia. The African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA), launched in January 2021, aims to enhance intra-African trade, making the adoption of the Pan-African Payment and Settlement System (PAPSS) crucial for reducing reliance on foreign currencies and fostering regional economic resilience.

articles.title: The National Dialogue’s Defining Test: Independence, Inclusion, and Trust
articles.summary: On June 24, the Ethiopian National Dialogue Commission (ENDC) marked its most significant milestone to date by officially unveiling eight core thematic agenda pillars. Ranging from state-building, the structure and form of government, and the status of federal cities, to peacebuilding, transitional justice, and human rights these pillars are intended to structurally anchor the highly […]
articles.context: The Ethiopian National Dialogue Commission was established in 2021 amid escalating ethnic tensions and conflict, particularly following the Tigray War that began in November 2020. The dialogue aims to address deep-rooted grievances and foster national unity, especially after the 2022 peace agreement between the Ethiopian government and Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF). The unveiling of the eight thematic pillars on June 24, 2023, marks a critical step in navigating Ethiopia's complex political landscape.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s Megaproject Push Leaves Legacy of Debt and Overexpansion, Officials Concede
articles.summary: Ethiopian officials have acknowledged that decades of state-led megaproject expansion left the country burdened with massive debt and underutilized infrastructure, prompting a shift toward a more commercially driven approach to public investment. Speaking on Ethiopia’s infrastructure and investment trajectory on the Ethiopia Delivers Summit this week, Ethiopian Investment Holdings (EIH) chief Brook Taye (PhD) said […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's ambitious infrastructure projects, particularly under the leadership of former Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed since 2018, aimed to transform the economy but have led to an estimated $30 billion in debt. The ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which erupted in November 2020, has further strained resources and diverted attention from economic development. This shift towards a commercially driven investment strategy reflects the urgent need for sustainable economic practices amid ongoing political and social challenges.

articles.title: Housing Market Study Set 11pct Benchmark for Annual Rent Increase
articles.summary: A residential rental market study commissioned by the Addis Ababa City Administration has concluded that maintaining annual consumer inflation below 10 percent could stabilize natural rent increases at 11.24 percent, providing what researchers describe as a key benchmark for managing the city’s rental market while reducing the need for aggressive state rent controls. The findings […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with inflation and economic instability, particularly following the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020, which disrupted supply chains and increased living costs. The Addis Ababa City Administration's study comes at a crucial time as the government seeks to balance economic recovery with housing affordability, especially amidst rising urban migration and a growing population in the capital. The proposed benchmark aims to mitigate tensions over housing while avoiding stringent rent controls.

articles.title: Integrating Refugees: Risk or Opportunity?
articles.summary: The burden of sheltering the multitudes fleeing conflict, insecurity, persecution, and climate shocks from across the Horn of Africa and beyond has long fallen upon Ethiopia. Today, with no fewer than 1.2 million refugees residing here, Ethiopia is home to one of the world’s largest refugee populations. Data indicates 43 percent of refugees are from […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been a key host for refugees due to its geographical location and historical ties with neighboring countries experiencing conflict, such as South Sudan and Somalia. The Tigray conflict, which escalated in November 2020, has further intensified the refugee crisis, with many fleeing violence and instability. Additionally, the 2021 peace agreement between the Ethiopian government and Tigray forces has created a complex political landscape, impacting both internal displacement and refugee integration efforts.

articles.title: Seismic Shifts in Global Politics Beckon New Chapter for Ethiopia-Canada Ties
articles.summary: Nicolas Simard is Canada’s ambassador to Ethiopia and Djibouti, as well as Head of Mission for Sudan. Before assuming his current role in July 2025, Simard served as ambassador to Mali, DRC, Nigeria, Bangladesh, in addition to a stint as director of planning and evaluation at the International Organization of La Francophonie, in Paris. His […]
articles.context: The recent appointment of Nicolas Simard as Canada’s ambassador to Ethiopia comes at a critical juncture following the signing of the Pretoria Agreement in November 2022, which aimed to end the Tigray conflict. As Ethiopia navigates post-conflict recovery and regional tensions with Sudan over border disputes, Canada’s renewed diplomatic engagement could play a pivotal role in fostering stability and addressing humanitarian needs in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: “Fundamentally Different”: AASTU President on Higher Learning, Industry 4.0
articles.summary: Founded in 2011 to advance Ethiopia’s engineering and scientific capabilities, Addis Ababa Science and Technology University (AASTU) has evolved into a leading specialized university. Today, it stands as one of Ethiopia’s premier higher learning institutions, driving progress in basic sciences and pioneering advancements at the frontiers of emerging technologies. Dereje Engida is a Chartered Engineer […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is currently navigating a complex political landscape marked by the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The government's focus on technological advancement and higher education, as emphasized by AASTU President Dereje Engida, is part of a broader strategy to foster national unity and economic recovery. This shift is crucial as Ethiopia seeks to rebuild and modernize its economy amid ongoing ethnic tensions and regional instability.

articles.title: حتى الشباب والأصحّاء يُنصحون بتجنب الجري والإفراط في شرب الكحول خلال موجات الحر
articles.summary: وسط موجة حار تشهدها المملكة المتحدة، يحذر الخبراء من تزايد حالات توقف القلب خلال فترات الطقس شديد الحرارة، مشيرين إلى أن تأثير الطقس الحار لن يؤثر فقط على كبار السن وذوي البنية الضعيفة فحسب، بل من الممكن أن يطال الأصحاء وأصحاب اللياقة البدنية.
articles.context: تأتي هذه التحذيرات في وقت تعاني فيه منطقة القرن الأفريقي، بما في ذلك الصومال، من آثار تغير المناخ، مما يزيد من حدة موجات الحر. في عام 2022، شهدت الصومال أسوأ موجة جفاف منذ عقود، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأزمات الصحية. مع تزايد الضغوط على النظام الصحي، يصبح من الضروري توعية الناس حول المخاطر الصحية المرتبطة بالحرارة، خاصة في ظل عدم الاستقرار السياسي والاقتصادي الذي تشهده البلاد.

articles.summary: The United States on Friday imposed sanctions on eight individuals and entities linked to networks The post US sanctions individuals, firms over support for Sudan’s war appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The sanctions come amid escalating violence in Sudan following the outbreak of conflict in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. articles.title: UNICEF demands truce, weapons ban to avert Sudan catastrophe

North Kordofan has become a focal point of this violence, with UNICEF reporting alarming child casualties, particularly from drone strikes. The situation is exacerbated by the lack of a ceasefire agreement, despite previous attempts, such as the Jeddah Process initiated in May 2023, to facilitate peace talks.

articles.title: Leer airstrip commissioned after WFP rehabilitation

The region has been severely affected by violence and displacement, with the United Nations reporting over 1.6 million people in need of assistance in South Sudan as of late 2022. The rehabilitation of the airstrip is crucial for facilitating humanitarian aid delivery, particularly as the country grapples with food insecurity and health emergencies exacerbated by conflict and flooding.

As South Sudan prepares for the FIBA Basketball World Cup 2027 African Qualifiers, this funding reflects the government's effort to foster national pride and unity through sports amid a backdrop of political uncertainty and social division.

articles.title: World Bank-Backed 3 Billion Birr Project to Revamp Power Distribution in Ethiopia

This major infrastructure initiative specifically targets the upgrading of utility networks in three critical […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's power distribution has faced significant challenges due to ongoing conflicts, particularly in the Tigray region, which has disrupted infrastructure and service delivery since the civil war began in November 2020. The Ethiopian Electric Utility's partnership with Alpha TND and Capital Electech, backed by World Bank funding, is crucial for restoring stability and improving energy access in the affected areas, aligning with the government's broader efforts to rebuild and foster economic recovery post-conflict.

The merger of Taza Legal LLP comes at a time when the Ethiopian government is emphasizing national sovereignty and local capacity building, especially in light of ongoing tensions in the Tigray region and the need for legal expertise to navigate complex issues arising from the conflict and economic recovery efforts.

articles.title: Last two POWs in Akobo to return home Saturday — Official
articles.summary: The last two prisoners of war held in Akobo are set to return to their families on Saturday, an official has confirmed, as authorities say all six detainees have been processed for repatriation through coordination with the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) and humanitarian partners. The involvement of the International Committee of the Red Cross underscores the critical role of international organizations in facilitating peace and reconciliation efforts in the region.

articles.title: Sudan: WFP warns fighting around El Obeid could deepen hunger crisis
articles.summary: The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) warned on Friday that escalating fighting around Sudan’s The post Sudan: WFP warns fighting around El Obeid could deepen hunger crisis appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .

The WFP's warning highlights the urgent need for humanitarian access amid a deteriorating situation, particularly as the rainy season approaches.

articles.title: Telecom operators defend tariff exchange rate adjustment
articles.summary: Mobile network operators say the adjustment of the telecommunications tariff exchange rate is intended to sustain reliable communication services amid rising operational costs. The National Communications Authority's approval of this adjustment comes amid a backdrop of rising operational costs exacerbated by the country's fragile political situation following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement, which aimed to stabilize the nation after years of civil conflict.

articles.title: SSPDF apologizes to civilians at civil-military dialogue in Nimule
articles.summary: A civil-military dialogue in Nimule has ended with the South Sudan People’s Defence Forces (SSPDF) publicly apologizing to civilians for past misconduct, according to the Office of the Mayor of Nimule. articles.context: The SSPDF's apology comes amid ongoing tensions in South Sudan following years of civil conflict that erupted in 2013, leading to widespread violence and human rights abuses. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to establish peace, but trust between the military and civilians remains fragile. The dialogue in Nimule is a critical step in addressing grievances and fostering reconciliation in a country still grappling with instability.

articles.title: No Bundibugyo Ebola cases recorded in South Sudan, Ministry and WHO Say
articles.summary: The Ministry of Health, in collaboration with the World Health Organization (WHO), has reassured the public that South Sudan has not recorded any confirmed cases of Bundibugyo Ebola virus disease, while emphasizing ongoing preparedness and surveillance measures across the country. The update was made during a joint press conference held in Juba, where health officials […] The post No Bundibugyo Ebola cases recorded in South Sudan, Ministry and WHO Say appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The recent reassurance from South Sudan's Ministry of Health comes amid heightened concerns over Ebola outbreaks in neighboring countries, particularly the Democratic Republic of the Congo, where Bundibugyo Ebola has been reported. Given South Sudan's fragile healthcare system, exacerbated by ongoing conflict since its independence in 2011, the collaboration with the WHO is crucial for effective surveillance and response measures, especially as the country prepares for the rainy season when disease transmission typically increases.

articles.title: Koang Pal Chang appointed to Media Authority board
articles.summary: Eye Radio Station Manager and Chief Editor Koang Pal Chang has been appointed as a board member of the national media regulator, the South Sudan Media Authority. articles.context: Koang Pal Chang's appointment comes amid ongoing efforts to stabilize South Sudan's media landscape following years of conflict and censorship. The South Sudan Media Authority, established in 2013, has faced challenges in promoting press freedom, especially during the civil war that erupted in 2013 and lasted until the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement. This appointment signals a potential shift towards greater media independence and accountability in a country striving for democratic governance.

articles.title: President Kiir honors Israeli veteran for role in liberation struggle

His involvement in the liberation struggle, supported by Israeli military expertise, highlights the historical ties between Israel and South Sudan. articles.title: Kenyan court bars media from covering deadly school fire trial
articles.summary: 8 girls face murder charges over dormitory blaze that killed 16 students

articles.context: South Sudan gained independence from Sudan in 2011 but has faced ongoing conflict, including a civil war that erupted in 2013.
